preview_text:
The model rates Belgium as a narrow favorite at 35%, only 2 percentage points ahead of Iran's 33% win probability. This is a tightly matched contest by the model's assessment, with Belgium holding a modest edge rather than clear superiority.

The draw probability of 32% reflects a competitive match in which neither side is heavily favored to force a decisive result. The three-way spread is relatively flat across the win outcomes, suggesting the model expects close competition with multiple plausible scorelines. A draw or an upset Iran victory remain live possibilities alongside a Belgium win.

The sportsbook consensus diverges substantially from the model. Books assign Belgium a 64% win probability—nearly 29 percentage points higher than the model—while compressing Iran's chances to 15%. The market also rates draws at 22%, well below the model's 32%. This represents a material gap; the dashboard does not adjudicate which view is correct, but the disagreement signals that bookmakers and the statistical forecast weigh the relative strength of these teams quite differently.
